programmes.show_description: No Refuge: Detective drama series. After four robberies in four days at the same house, Frost comes up with a surprising arrest as a result of lateral thinking.
Brew It Yourself: Enduring '70s sitcom about a chirpy driver and his conductor pal at the Luxton Bus Company. Stan turns up to work drunk after sampling too much homemade beer.
Your daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection.
Shopping from home.
Moving On: Classic British sitcom. Simon and Oliver are worried about their own situation and not seeing their grandson when David and Lyn drop hints about moving.
Going Steady: Enduring '70s British sitcom about a chirpy bus driver and his conductor pal. Stan's attempts to woo the inspector's niece at a dinner party end in disaster.
Gennie introduces Shadrach to her mother as her biological father. Val bars the Dingles from the Woolpack and Viv creates a wanted poster to help her find Freddie.
Bob worries when Viv starts having nightmares about Freddie. Gennie and her mother visit Shadrach, and Katie is disappointed with the turnout for her engagement party.
Following a tip-off from Lauren, an incredulous Sean races to stop Violet and Jamie leaving with baby Dylan, while Tina shows her disgust for David's declaration of love.
Gail warns Tina about messing with David, but the girl delivers shocking news. Michelle is reluctant to let Alex leave The Rovers, and Sean realises he may never see his son again.
Paying the Price: Drama series. A ruthless kidnapper tests Frost's skills to the full in a case that proves to be tough to crack and where time is running out for one woman.
You Never Can Tell: Foul play is suspected when an ageing pop manager dies after returning to the village to marry a young starlet, and Peggy experiences a spell of memory loss.
Mixed Messages: Carol turns to Joe for help when a pensioner she has been visiting regularly dies unexpectedly and suspicions are aroused, and Gina's pregnancy approaches full term.
Beyond the Grave: Barnaby and Troy try to find out whether mysterious events in Aspern Tallow and the discovery of a body are supernatural or if there is a rational explanation.
Donna and Ross agree to help Viv's search. Nicola has a disappointingly quiet 30th birthday and her day gets even worse when Paddy lays her off. Doug reassures Greg.
Gail urges Tina not to make a rash decision, and suggests she should not tell David her news just yet, and there's friction at The Rovers when Wendy comes to collect Alex.
Steve expresses disapproval of Michelle's pandering to Alex, and Jack grows suspicious of Paul, while Tina worries about having to wait for an appointment at the clinic.
Oscar's Birthday: Everyone rallies around for Blaketon's birthday party, and two new arrivals in the village put Joe in an awkward position both professionally and personally.
Family Matters: Sergeant Miller's daughter is kidnapped, and Mason and Wetherby struggle to deal with student protests against a French delegate who collaborated with the Nazis.
Darkwater: DCI Vera Stanhope must scrutinise the fractured relationships of a community when the body of a teenage boy is found in a reservoir in a remote rural town.
Oracle: Crime drama series. Endeavour sees in 1970 at a Venetian opera and meets a kindred spirit as a grisly murder on an Oxford towpath speaks to Thursday's intuition.
Paying the Price: Drama series. A ruthless kidnapper tests Frost's skills to the full in a case that proves to be tough to crack and where time is running out for one woman.
Busmen's Perks: Enduring '70s sitcom about a chirpy bus driver and his conductor pal. Stan's household is thrown into disarray when he decides to do a spot of home decorating.
Your daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection.
Shopping from home.
It's a Long Way to Friern Barnet: Classic British sitcom. David and Lyn settle into a new home as Simon and Oliver compete to ensure they have all the domestic essentials they need.
The Squeeze: Enduring '70s sitcom. Stan and Jack try to convince the inspector to buy an old motorbike belonging to Arthur, but the test drive does not go quite as they planned.
Donna and Ross agree to help Viv's search. Nicola has a disappointingly quiet 30th birthday and her day gets even worse when Paddy lays her off. Doug reassures Greg.
Gail urges Tina not to make a rash decision, and suggests she should not tell David her news just yet, and there's friction at The Rovers when Wendy comes to collect Alex.
Steve expresses disapproval of Michelle's pandering to Alex, and Jack grows suspicious of Paul, while Tina worries about having to wait for an appointment at the clinic.
Unknown Soldiers: Frost investigates a soldier's death, a security van robbery, and himself being held at gunpoint by a drug dealer when he is convinced that they are linked.
Oscar's Birthday: Everyone rallies around for Blaketon's birthday party, and two new arrivals in the village put Joe in an awkward position both professionally and personally.
Family Matters: Sergeant Miller's daughter is kidnapped, and Mason and Wetherby struggle to deal with student protests against a French delegate who collaborated with the Nazis.
Garden of Death: Feature-length drama. A matriarchal family dig up more than just the animosity of local villagers when they turn Midsomer Deverell's Memorial Garden into a teashop.
Ashley attends the hearing alone and is worried to see his wife with Greg upon his return. Nicola takes an opportunity to help out Paddy, and Viv and Bob are freed on bail.
Viv is devastated when she realises even Bob is questioning her innocence after they are freed on bail. Ashley and Laurel regret going out, and Nicola manipulates Paddy.
Ryan opens up to Liam, prompting him to convince Michelle to reconsider her position with Alex. Dan finds an odd way to make his point to Paul, and Tina learns more about David.
Michelle tells a devastated Alex to go home with Wendy. Kirk is struggling to find a job, while Claire becomes increasingly worried about Chesney's well-being.
England Expects: An old villain returns to Aidensfield with a score to settle with the police, and Peggy's persuasion of David to start making clay pots has explosive consequences.
Mother of Invention: Joe and Carol join forces to support a struggling single mother, and Ashfordly Police are put to task when the local police constable's wife loses her dog.
Looking Good Dead: Crime drama series. Grace and Branson find themselves tested to the limit when two seemingly unconnected deaths may be the work of a twisted serial killer.
Raga: Crime drama series. The lead-up to 1970's general election sees racial tensions escalate in Oxford and a tragedy that tests family loyalties strike in an Indian restaurant.
